reasoning: `container_wf` explicitly asserts `self.container_map_2m@.dom().subset_of(self.allocated_pages_4k@)` — the domain of the 2m container map is checked against 4k allocated pages rather than 2m allocated pages. The comment `@Xiangdong Come back for this` confirms this is known incomplete. This is a copy-paste bug from the `container_map_4k` line.

Same copy-paste pattern: `container_wf` asserts `self.container_map_1g@.dom().subset_of(self.allocated_pages_4k@)` — 1g container mappings are checked against 4k allocated pages instead of 1g allocated pages. The developer comment and identical pattern confirm this is a bug.

`PageTable::wf` is `closed spec fn` with `#[verifier::external_body]` — its body is opaque and unverified. All properties linking structural page table fields to ghost mappings are trusted axioms, creating a soundness gap where incorrect implementations could satisfy the spec vacuously.

`thread_inv` is an `#[verifier::external_body]` proof fn — its ensures clause (threads have valid owning_container and owning_proc) is assumed without verification. This cross-component invariant is a critical soundness dependency that Verus never checks.
